channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Japan’s ruling party HQ attacked with firebombs; man arrested: Reports
item.description: A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s ruling party in Tokyo Saturday, media outlets report.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama/The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Hezbollah says its war with Israel is entering a new phase
item.description: Lebanon’s Hezbollah militant group said Friday it is entering a new phase in its fight against invading Israeli troops, as the region reckons with the killing of top Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar in a battle with Israeli forces in Gaza. Hamas acknowledged Sinwar’s death and described him as a martyr.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea claims it discovered the remains of a South Korean drone in its capital
item.description: North Korea says it found the remains of a South Korean drone during a search in its capital, Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea troops readying for Ukraine combat: South Korea
item.description: South Korea said it's used facial recognition AI tech to identify North Korean officers in eastern Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Ju-min Park, NewsNation

item.title: UN expands arms embargo on Haiti to all types of arms and ammunition
item.description: The U.N. Security Council has voted unanimously to expand the arms embargo in Haiti to all types of weapons and ammunition, expressing grave concern at the extremely high levels of gang violence and criminal activities in the impoverished Caribbean nation.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Palau’s vibrant Helen Reef is a magnet for poachers. These rangers keep it safe
item.description: Helen Reef, a 40-square-mile atoll at the southern edge of Palau, is one of Earth's most remote and biologically diverse reefs.
item.dc:creator: Yannick Peterhans, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia’s war in Ukraine
item.description: South Korea's spy agency said North Korea has sent troops to support Russia's war in Ukraine, transferring 1,500 North Korean special operation forces to the Russian city of Vladivostok.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two Venezuelan men shot in head in Juarez
item.description: Police in Juarez, Mexico, are investigating a shooting that left one Venezuelan dead, and another critically injured on Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Gangs attack neighborhoods across Haiti's capital in new wave of violence
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Multiple gangs attacked several communities in the Haitian capital of Port-au-Prince on Friday, setting fire to homes and a church as bullets whizzed through the area. At least one woman was killed as gangs opened fire in Solino, St.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sinwar's ruthless brother Mohammed slated to take over as leader of Hamas
item.description: Mohammed Sinwar is expected to take over as leader of Hamas after his older brother Yahya Sinwar was confirmed to have been killed in Gaza by the Israel Defense Forces as the brutal war continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK Foreign Secretary Lammy urges China against supporting Russia's military
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Britain's foreign secretary raised concerns about China's support of Russia in its war against Ukraine on Friday, urging his Chinese counterpart to prevent Chinese firms from supplying Russia's military in a meeting in Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Whelan says Brittney Griner was 'great help' in advocating for his freedom
item.description: Former U.S. Marine Paul Whelan said WNBA star Brittney Griner was of “great help” in advocating for his freedom after being detained in Russia since 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ex-Indian government employee charged in US assassination plot
item.description: The Justice Department announced charges against a former Indian government employee in the murder plot against dual U.S.-Canadian citizen Gurpatwant Singh Pannun, a prominent Sikh separatist.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: South Korean intelligence says North has sent troops to aid Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's spy agency said Friday that North Korea has dispatched troops to support Russia's war against Ukraine. If confirmed, the move would bring a third country into the war and intensify a standoff between North Korea and the West.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Military voters see 'direct attack' from GOP election challenges
item.description: Republicans are coming under fire from veterans and military groups for attempting to restrict the votes of overseas service members through a series of lawsuits in battleground states.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Israel needs to commit to Gaza rebuilding: Gen. David Petraeus
item.description: A lasting peace will depend on the will of the Israeli coalition government, said the retired Army general and former CIA director.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says it's time to 'move on' in call with Netanyahu after Sinwar killing
item.description: President Biden congratulated Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Israel’s killing of Hamas chief Yahya Sinwar, the architect of the Oct. 7, 2023, attack that triggered a yearlong war and introduced new tensions between the leaders.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Harris: Israeli killing of Sinwar an opportunity to end war in Gaza
item.description: Vice President Harris on Thursday praised Israel's killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar, calling for it to be an opportunity for the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza to end after more than a year of fighting in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine's former armed forces chief endorses 'victory plan' in first speech since his dismissal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ukraine's former commander-in-chief, Valerii Zaluzhnyi, on Thursday expressed his support for President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s "victory plan," despite the tensions between the two that led to his dismissal.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas terror leader Yahya Sinwar killed in Gaza, Israel says
item.description: Israeli officials announced Thursday that Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar was killed during military operations inside the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An independent journalist arrested on treason charges in Belarus
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — An independent journalist in Belarus has been arrested on charges of high treason, the latest move in a relentless crackdown on media and free speech by authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko's government, a media rights watchdog said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bangladesh court issues arrest warrant for ex-Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina over deaths of protesters
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — A special court in Bangladesh issued arrest warrants on Thursday for former Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina and 45 others, including her close aides, on charges of crimes against humanity during a student-led uprising in July and August that forced her to flee the country, a pr
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy outlines his 'victory plan' to Ukraine's lawmakers, including a call to join NATO
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy told lawmakers Wednesday that Ukraine’s Western partners are increasing pressure to negotiate with Russia, but he hinted such talks would be unfavorable to Kyiv as he unveiled what he called his “victory plan” for the war.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taliban begins enforcing rule banning 'images of living beings' in Afghan media
item.description: The Taliban is preventing certain outlets from airing images of human beings or animals, according to officials. The rule pertains to certain provinces at the time being.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Christmas Island National Park travel guide where millions of colorful crabs migrate annually
item.description: From diving to birdwatching, there is a plethora of experiences to take part in at Christmas Island National Park. The red crab migration is one popular experience for tourists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden to travel to Germany Thursday, delays African leg of trip
item.description: In Berlin, Biden will convene a meeting with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz to discuss continued support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 27 in Lebanon, including in a town with a dark history of civilian deaths
item.description: QANA, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes pounded areas across Lebanon, killing at least 27 people over the past 24 hours, officials said Wednesday, including more than a dozen in a southern town where Israeli bombardments in previous conflicts are seared into local memory.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD ZAATARI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A look at the oldest stone tools ever discovered, dating back 3.3M years
item.description: The oldest stone tools ever discovered were unearthed in Kenya by a group of scientists at the Lomekwi 3 site. The stone tools unearthed are simple in their design and pre-date humans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Australian police sergeant likely to be charged over a Nazi salute
item.description: An Australian state police chief apologized to the Jewish community after a sergeant allegedly performed a Nazi salute.
item.dc:creator: Rod McGuirk, The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah vows to expand attacks in Israel after deadly strike in Lebanon's Christian heartland
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — The day after a deadly Israeli airstrike in northern Lebanon – far from Hezbollah’s main area of influence – the militant group's acting leader said it would aim rockets into more areas of Israel.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strike in northern Lebanon kills at least 21 people
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit an apartment building in northern Lebanon on Monday, killing at least 21 people, according to the Lebanese Red Cross. The Israeli military did not immediately comment and the target was not clear.
item.dc:creator: YAHYA HABCHITI, KAREEM CHEHAYEB, and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UN Security Council voices 'strong concern' for UN peacekeepers after Israeli attacks
item.description: It's the first statement by the powerful U.N. body since Israel's attacks on the positions of the peacekeeping force.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. to send troops and air defense system to Israel
item.description: The Pentagon announced the U.S. military will send a Terminal High Altitude Defense battery and about 100 U.S. troops to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: Israel says 4 soldiers killed by Hezbollah drone attack while Israeli strike in Gaza leaves 20 dead
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hezbollah drone attack on an army base in central Israel killed four soldiers and severely wounded seven others Sunday, the military said, in the deadliest strike by the militant group since Israel launched its ground invasion of Lebanon nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
